content.claim: Church schools in Hungary serve a more socioeconomically advantaged student intake on average compared to public schools, with family-background status index of 0.18 in church schools versus minus 0.05 in public schools at grade 6 in 2017
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Data point is from single year (2017) and may not reflect current composition
Index construction and threshold for 'advantaged' status not fully specified
Causal mechanism for differential intake (selection, location, affordability) not determined
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
